title: Browser Trust, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Headers · Type-System Trace: Csrf Token Origin Fetch Metadata
summary: A browser API reflects origins, combines credentials with wildcard sharing, treats CORS as access control, relies only on SameSite, and emits weak cookie and framing policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts csrf token origin fetch metadata increment in browser request and response-sharing policy matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Protect cookie-authenticated mutations with safe-method semantics, tokens, trusted Origin or Referer, Fetch Metadata, and explicit exceptional-client handling.
Separate browser response-sharing controls from server authentication, authorization, network clients, and cross-origin request sending.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
